item.title: Imaging in inflammatory bowel disease 2025: ECCO-ESGAR-ESP-IBUS diagnostic and monitoring recommendations with MRI and intestinal ultrasound in treat-to-target strategies.
item.description: Insights into imaging - Review - The 2025 ECCO-ESGAR-ESP-IBUS multisociety guidelines mark a paradigm shift in IBD management, positioning imaging as a central element of the treat-to-target strategy. This critical review analyzes...

item.title: A novel super-resolution contrast-enhanced ultrasound approach for evaluating inflammatory activity in Crohn's disease.
item.description: Insights into imaging - To investigate the role of super-resolution contrast-enhanced ultrasound (SR-CEUS) in evaluating inflammatory activity in Crohn's disease (CD). In this prospective study, we consecutively enrolled ...

item.title: Transabdominal intestinal ultrasound parameters as intermediate treatment targets in a treat-to-target strategy for moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Intestinal research - This study aimed to investigate the prognostic value of early post-induction intestinal ultrasound (IUS) findings in predicting long-term clinical outcomes among patients with moderate-to-severe ul...

item.title: Early transmural response assessed by intestinal ultrasound predicts long-term clinical remission in patients with IBD: Results from the TRUST BEYOND study.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The study sought to prospectively examine clinical, biochemical, and transmural outcomes. Transmural outcomes are assessed by ultrasound after 1 year in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IB...

item.title: Bowel Wall Thickness Measured by Intestinal Ultrasound as a Marker of Endoscopic Disease Activity in Patients with Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Gastroenterologia y hepatologia - To investigate the correlation between bowel wall thickness (BWT) measured by intestinal ultrasound (IUS) and endoscopic disease activity in patients with Crohn's disease (CD), and to compare its d...

item.title: Revolutionizing Crohn's disease detection: integrating AI with intestinal ultrasound for superior diagnosis.
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastroenterology - Intestinal ultrasound (IUS) is increasingly utilized by healthcare providers for the diagnosis of Crohn's disease (CD), which has highlighted new challenges related to standardized image interpreta...
